THE MOTION TO QUASH IS GRANTED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO SFUSD SERVING NEW RECORDS SUBPOENAS THAT ARE MORE NARROWLY TAILORED TO DISCOVER ONLY DIRECTLY RELEVANT INFORMATION. SANCTIONS REQUESTS ARE DENIED AS BOTH SIDES HAD SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE MINI-MINUTES AND ORDER FOR COMPLETE RULING.) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489. (302/HK)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-09-2015 Off Calendar

Plaintiff Veronica Chavez'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Or Limit In Part Overbroad Personnel Records Subpoenas Or, In The Alternative, Motion For A Protective Order
Off Calendar Dec-14-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Moira McQuaid of Law Offices of Moira McQuaid telephone number 650-638-2317 for Plaintiff Veronica Chavez.
Kathleen Darmagnac of Stubbs & Leone telephone number 925-974-8600 for Defendant San Francisco Unified School District.

PLAINTIFF VERONICA CHAVEZ'S Motion To Quash Or Limit In Part Overbroad Personnel Records Subpoenas Or, In The Alternative, Motion For A Protective Order - The Court adopted the recommendation of the Judge Pro Tem. The motion to quash is granted without prejudice to SFUSD serving new records subpoenas that are more narrowly tailored to discover only directly relevant information. The applications and other materials submitted to the other school districts, and their action thereon, constitute employment records protected by the right to privacy. The subpoenas are overbroad and seek all records without limitation. SFUSD has not shown how that job applications and other records it seeks held by prospective employers are"directly relevant" to plaintiff's claims. "The proponent of discovery of constitutionally protected material has the burden of making a threshold showing that the evidence sought is 'directly relevant' to the claim or defense." (Harris v. Superior Court (1992) 3 Cal.App.4th 661, 665). Sanctions requests are denied as both sides had substantial justification. This is a discovery matter and the court will not provide a court reporter. If the parties desire a reporter, they shall meet and confer and agree upon one prior to the hearing. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Or Limit In Part Overbroad Medical Records Subpoenas Or, In The Alternative, Motion For A Protective Order; And A Motion For Sanctions Against Defendant
Off Calendar Dec-08-2015 Continued To Dec-14-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Moira McQuaid of Law Offices of Moira C. McQuaid telephone number 650-638-2317 for Plaintiff.
Kathleen Darmagnac and Sarah Castelhano of Stubbs & Leone telephone number 925-974-8600 for Defendant.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court vacated its tentative rulings. The Parties are to work on/draft a protective order that is agreeable to both sides with respect to medical and mental health records being provided to only those who need to know.
The following matters:

1) PLAINTIFF VERONICA CHAVEZ'S Motion To Quash Or Limit In Part Overbroad Medical Records Subpoenas Or, In The Alternative, Motion For A Protective Order; And A Motion For Sanctions

2) PLAINTIFF VERONICA CHAVEZ'S Motion To Quash Or Limit In Part Overbroad Personnel Records Subpoenas Or, In The Alternative, Motion For A Protective Order

3) Defendant San Francisco Unified School District's Motion For Order Compelling Plaintiffs Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents; Request For Sanctions Of $2,200.00 Against Plaintiffs Attorney Of Record, Moira Mcquaid Pursuant To CCP Sec. 2031.310(H)

are continued to December 14, 2015 at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302 for the parties to come in with a proposed order.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489. (302/HK)
